dependencies: Some suppliers or their sub-suppliers are limited- or sole-source suppliers
Ability to meet obligations to customers depends on product quality, performance, continued product availability and stability of such suppliers
Supply-constrained environment impacting industry for past several years
Significant input shortages, supplier capacity constraints, supplier or customer production disruptions
Complex supply chains extending into many different countries and regions
Dependencies on certain key internal manufacturing or other facilities
risks: Supply chain shortages and disruptions continue to pose challenges
Disruptions from war, natural disasters, public health pandemics, governmental actions
Climate change impacts (sea level rise, drought, flooding, wildfires, intense weather events)
Cyber-attacks on suppliers negatively impacting delivery obligations

regulatory_risks: U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) design and production regulations
European Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) regulations
Type certificate and production certificate requirements
Repair station certificates from multiple aviation regulators
Regulatory oversight with potential for fines, suspension or revocation of certificates
Aviation authorities' power to mandate inspections, maintenance, modifications or grounding of aircraft fleets
Export controls (U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR) and International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR))
Import controls
Sanctions compliance (U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC))
Anti-boycott regulations
Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS) for U.S. government contracts
Procurement, import/export, security, contract pricing and cost requirements for government contracts
Environmental protection laws (discharge, treatment, storage, disposal, remediation of hazardous substances)
Climate change regulations (emissions caps, carbon pricing and taxes, energy taxes, product fuel efficiency standards)
Mandatory climate disclosure obligations including U.S. government procurement-related contractual climate disclosures
Increased cybersecurity requirements
Data privacy and sovereignty regulations
Artificial intelligence regulations
Anti-corruption laws
Competition law
Public procurement law
Technical or local content regulations
PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) restrictions
Currency controls
Changes to tax laws (global minimum taxes, taxation of global income)
OECD Pillar 2 global minimum tax of 15%
U.S. Inflation Reduction Act (corporate alternative minimum tax, excise tax on stock buybacks)
Potential changes in U.S. and foreign trade, economic policies following recent U.S. elections
Tariffs, export controls, restrictions on outbound investment
Trade barriers and sanctions
Protectionism, economic nationalism trends

sanction_risks: Russia invasion of Ukraine and related sanctions imposed by EU, U.S. and other countries
Escalation of sanctions between U.S. and China or other countries
Tariffs and trade tensions including intensified decoupling between U.S. and China
Export controls and restrictions on outbound investment
Conflict in Middle East posing risk of wider regional conflict
